themeWords.meaning: rebellion, breach of trust; transgression against God or man
fire; often symbolic of divine judgment

sentenceTheme: God pronounces judgment on Moab, Judah, and Israel for their transgressions, detailing their sins and declaring unavoidable consequences.

verses.text: Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Moab, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because he burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime:

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel, the LORD
Moab; a nation descended from Lot, east of the Dead Sea

verses.text: But I will send a fire upon Moab, and it shall devour the palaces of Kirioth: and Moab shall die with tumult, with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet:

highlightedWords.meaning: fire; often symbolic of divine judgment
Kirioth; a town or complex of towns in Moab

verses.text: And I will cut off the judge from the midst thereof, and will slay all the princes thereof with him, saith the LORD.

highlightedWords.meaning: judge, ruler; one who decides or governs
the personal name of the God of Israel, the LORD

verses.text: Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Judah, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they have despised the law of the LORD, and have not kept his commandments, and their lies caused them to err, after the which their fathers have walked:

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom and tribe of Israel

verses.text: But I will send a fire upon Judah, and it shall devour the palaces of Jerusalem.

highlightedWords.meaning: fire; often symbolic of divine judgment
Jerusalem; the chief city of Israel/Judah

verses.text: Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Israel, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes;

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; the covenant people and nation
righteous, just; one in the right

verses.text: That pant after the dust of the earth on the head of the poor, and turn aside the way of the meek: and a man and his father will go in unto the same maid, to profane my holy name:
And they lay themselves down upon clothes laid to pledge by every altar, and they drink the wine of the condemned in the house of their god.
Yet destroyed I the Amorite before them, whose height was like the height of the cedars, and he was strong as the oaks; yet I destroyed his fruit from above, and his roots from beneath.
Also I brought you up from the land of Egypt, and led you forty years through the wilderness, to possess the land of the Amorite.

highlightedWords.meaning: Egypt; the land of the Nile

verses.text: And I raised up of your sons for prophets, and of your young men for Nazarites. Is it not even thus, O ye children of Israel? saith the LORD.
But ye gave the Nazarites wine to drink; and commanded the prophets, saying, Prophesy not.

highlightedWords.meaning: to prophesy, speak by divine inspiration

verses.text: Behold, I am pressed under you, as a cart is pressed that is full of sheaves.
Therefore the flight shall perish from the swift, and the strong shall not strengthen his force, neither shall the mighty deliver himself:
Neither shall he stand that handleth the bow; and he that is swift of foot shall not deliver himself: neither shall he that rideth the horse deliver himself.
And he that is courageous among the mighty shall flee away naked in that day, saith the LORD.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel, the LORD
